what_it_does: It is a posted noticeboard note (a comment) in the dragnet-development channel, recording Tim's design point about radial UI verbs versus sheet-style dwell-places and where stable-identity actions/modes belong.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-2ec3cda2, address board://item-2ec3cda2, type note, source claude_code, state posted, title "Tim · point", author_session tim, channel dragnet-development.
It links via commented_on to board://item-99a1d803 and has a single history entry showing filing at 2026-06-24T08:23:50.085073+00:00.
Body is a [point] response to a prior note that warns against over-radializing and assigns radial = verbs, sheet = dwell-places.
Tim agrees not everything should be forced into the radial V, expects other slots to appear as 'icon cons' and components on the page, but objects to using sheets for actions or modes.
The note proposes a heuristic: sheets are appropriate for document lists (dwell-places) but not for things with a stable identity.
No code symbols are present; the content is conversational design rationale.
standouts: The radial/sheet taxonomy: radial = verbs, sheet = dwell-places.
The pushback: stable-identity actions and modes should not live in sheets.
It is a threaded comment on item-99a1d803, preserving a design debate as a ledger item.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter, id/address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, history.
Uses a [point] marker to label the comment's rhetorical role.
Conversational, unedited prose typical of channel-memory capture.
concerns: The argument is tentative and vague ('I don't quite understand', 'I don't know how to describe it', 'Maybe I'm oversimplifying'), so the design rule is under-specified.
Term 'icon cons' is ambiguous (likely 'icon containers' or a typo) and not defined in the file.
No concrete examples or acceptance criteria for 'stable identity' are given.
It responds to prior context (the V, radial, sheets) that is not included here.
notes: Tim's exploratory design note argues that actions/modes with stable identity belong in persistent page components, not sheets, while sheets are fine for document lists.
questions: What exactly is the 'V' and the radial UI being discussed?
What are the 'other slots' and how do they relate to the radial/sheet layout?
How is 'stable identity' defined for UI elements?
What did item-99a1d803 say in full?
purpose_vs_actual: It is filed as a note/comment and behaves as one: it records a design point and links it to a prior item. The actual content is more exploratory than a settled decision, so it reads as a design-in-progress note rather than a finalized rule.
apparent_themes: Stable identity vs transient containers in UI architecture.
Avoiding sheet-based switching for actions and modes.
Design constraint: don't overload the radial V.
Capturing informal design rationale in a ledger.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to refine the radial/sheet boundary by insisting that persistent, identity-bearing actions and modes remain as first-class page components/radial verbs, while reserving sheets for list-like, document-oriented content.
novelty: The 'stable identity shouldn't be in sheets' heuristic, and the candid, half-formed reasoning captured as a first-class design artifact.
contribution: It contributes a specific design position—stable-identity actions/modes belong outside sheets—and a nuance to the radial/sheet taxonomy; it also contributes traceability (author, timestamp, channel, commented_on link) for the design conversation.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by tim in dragnet-development commenting on item-99a1d803, arguing that actions and modes with stable identity should not be placed in sheets while sheets remain appropriate for document-like lists.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate a design position on the boundary between radial UI verbs and sheet-based dwell-places.
